# filejson

> Zero-config automatic JSON file persistence for Node.js with atomic writes, debouncing, and crash safety.

## What it does

`filejson` wraps a plain JavaScript object in an ES6 Proxy. Any mutation to
`instance.contents` (set, delete, nested assignment) is automatically detected
and written back to a JSON file on disk — no explicit save calls required for
normal usage.

## Installation

```
npm install filejson
```

## Core API

### `new Filejson(cfg?)`

Constructor. Accepts an optional configuration object:

| Option         | Type    | Default | Description                                           |
|----------------|---------|---------|-------------------------------------------------------|
| `filename`     | string  | `""`    | Path to the JSON file (also set by `load()`).         |
| `space`        | number  | `2`     | JSON indentation spaces passed to `JSON.stringify`.   |
| `verbose`      | boolean | `false` | Log save/load events to stdout.                       |
| `saveDelay`    | number  | `100`   | Debounce delay in milliseconds before writing to disk.|
| `atomicWrites` | boolean | `true`  | Write to a temp file then rename (crash-safe).        |

### `instance.load(filename, [overwriteWith], [callback])` → `Promise | void`

Reads and parses the JSON file at `filename`, sets `instance.contents` to the
parsed value, and stores `filename` for future saves. Optionally overwrites
the file with `overwriteWith`. Returns a Promise when no callback is supplied.

### `instance.save([callback])` → `Promise | void`

Debounced async save. Clears any pending timer and schedules a new write after
`cfg.saveDelay` ms (default 100 ms). Uses atomic writes by default
(write-to-temp + rename). Returns a Promise when no callback is supplied.

### `instance.saveSync()` → `instance`

Synchronous save. Cancels any pending debounced save and writes immediately.
Intended for use in process exit handlers where async operations may not
complete. Throws on serialization or I/O errors.

### `instance.contents`

The live JavaScript object that is persisted. Any property assignment or
deletion on this object (including deeply nested paths) automatically triggers
`save()`.

### `instance.paused`

Boolean. Set to `true` to temporarily suppress automatic saves while making
bulk mutations; set back to `false` when done. `load()` sets this internally
during initialisation.

## Usage patterns

### Callback style
```js
const Filejson = require('filejson');
const file = new Filejson();

file.load('./data.json', function(err, f) {
  if (err) throw err;
  f.contents.counter = (f.contents.counter || 0) + 1; // auto-saved
});
```

### Async/await style
```js
const Filejson = require('filejson');
const file = new Filejson();

const f = await file.load('./data.json');
f.contents.name = 'Alice'; // auto-saved after 100 ms
```

### Overwrite file on load
```js
const f = await file.load('./data.json', { version: 1 });
```

### Exit handler (synchronous save)
```js
process.on('SIGINT', () => {
  file.saveSync();
  process.exit(0);
});
```

### Bulk mutations (pause auto-save)
```js
file.paused = true;
file.contents.a = 1;
file.contents.b = 2;
file.contents.c = 3;
file.paused = false;
await file.save(); // single write for all three changes
```

### Custom debounce / disable atomic writes
```js
const file = new Filejson({ saveDelay: 500, atomicWrites: false });
```

## Caveats

- The JSON file **must exist** before calling `load()` unless `overwriteWith`
  is provided to create/overwrite it.
- Only JSON-compatible types are allowed in `contents`
  (string, number, boolean, object, array, null). Assigning functions or
  Symbols throws an error.
- `save()` is debounced: rapid successive mutations result in a single disk
  write after the delay. If you need an immediate write use `saveSync()` or
  await `save()` and then check that the delay has elapsed.
- Concurrent async saves are serialised internally; if a write is in progress a
  new `save()` call reschedules itself rather than overlapping.
- TypeScript types are available via the bundled `index.d.ts`.

## Repository

https://github.com/bchr02/filejson
